name: Ginger

book_claims.claim: Speeds up gastric emptying, promotes satiety and increases thermic effect of food, helping prevent obesity
book_claims.mechanism: Active molecule gingerol (converted to zingerone when cooked). Studies in healthy men and women showed these effects.

book_claims.claim: May reduce nausea and vomiting during chemotherapy
book_claims.mechanism: Gingerol's effect on digestive tract motility and nausea receptors

supplementary_research.finding: Systematic review and meta-analysis (Saneei et al., 2019) of 14 RCTs found ginger supplementation significantly reduced fasting blood glucose and HbA1c in type 2 diabetes patients, supporting anti-diabetic effects.

Meta-analysis of 12 RCTs (Mazidi et al., 2016) found ginger supplementation significantly reduced CRP and TNF-alpha levels, confirming systemic anti-inflammatory effects relevant to chronic disease prevention.

Examine's evidence summary rates ginger as having strong evidence for anti-nausea effects (pregnancy, chemotherapy, post-operative), moderate evidence for pain reduction, and notable evidence for reducing inflammatory markers and blood glucose.

Cochrane review (Viljoen et al., 2014, updated) confirmed ginger is effective for reducing nausea and vomiting in early pregnancy with no significant side effects, supporting the book's claim about anti-nausea properties.

Meta-analysis of 6 RCTs (Maharlouei et al., 2019) found ginger supplementation significantly reduced body weight and waist-to-hip ratio in overweight/obese subjects, supporting the book's obesity prevention claim.

culinary_pairings.source: Classic pairing in teas and dressings
Thai curry pastes and Indian masalas
Warming spice blends (garam masala, ras el hanout)
Classic Chinese aromatic trinity with garlic

seasonality: Available year-round; fresh ginger peaks in autumn harvest but is stored and shipped globally. Young ginger (spring ginger) available seasonally with milder flavor.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Converted to zingerone and shogaol when cooked or dried; shogaol is more potent. Lipophilic, absorption improved with fats.

synergies.description: Both are rhizomes. Ginger's COX-2 pathway complements turmeric's NF-kB pathway for broader anti-inflammatory coverage.
